is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum DHFR and DHPS gene polymorphisms in isolates from Tanzanian children; inclusion required monoinfection with P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports DHFR codons 51, 59, 108 and DHPS codons 436, 437, 540, 581, 613 — all curated target positions (Tables 1 and 2).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 and Table 2 give counts and percentages per codon, e.g. 51-asparagine 14 (31.1%) of n=45; 540-glutamic acid 36 (80%).

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples collected in the village of Magoda, Tanga Region, north-east Tanzania — a specific named village below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Frequencies binned by year: 1995 (n=45) and 1996 (n=27 day 0, n=26 day 7), each a single collection year (Table 2).

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children recruited in Magoda, Tanga Region, Tanzania; blood taken before treatment. Lab strains only used as PCR controls.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical field study with two therapeutic studies conducted in 1995 and 1996.

duplicate_risk.evidence: DHFR results for the 1995 isolates were already published in Jelinek et al. 1997 (Trop Med Int Health 2,1075-1079); studies were within the same population as a 1994 study (Rønn et al. 1996).

supplement.evidence: Full per-codon frequency counts are provided in Tables 1 and 2 in the main text.

assessment.summary: Field study in Magoda village, Tanga Region, Tanzania (1995 and 1996) reporting DHFR (51,59,108) and DHPS (436,437,540,581,613) codon frequencies per year in P. falciparum isolates from children. Extractable counts/percentages given by year and by day 0/day 7 in Tables 1 and 2. All criteria met. Note DHFR 1995 data previously published (Jelinek et al. 1997), giving medium duplicate risk.
